assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Sub-task required: Yes'. subtask-30001.md exists with the transaction wrapping sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Sub-task required: Yes'. subtask-30002.md exists with the index addition sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and '## Sub-task required: No'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and '## Sub-task required: No'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20). subtask-30002.md likewise contains: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 33: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-30002.md line 37: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md has '## Review Context' (line 28) with quoted original comment: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'. subtask-30002.md has '## Review Context' (line 29) with quoted original comment: '> The migration should also add an index on `deleted_at` for the sbom table...' including the SQL code block.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), sub-task descriptions (subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md), and the report (report.md). No source code files appear in the outputs. The report states 'This report was generated by the verify-pr skill' and contains no indication of code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report references all four: '4 review comments classified: 2 code change requests (sub-tasks created), 1 nit, 1 question'. Each comment is addressed in the report body with numbered items 1-4.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall status is 'WARN' (line 17), and it states 'Two code change requests from reviewer-a require follow-up sub-tasks before merge' (line 19). There is no mention of merging, approving, or any merge action being taken. The report explicitly blocks merge by requiring sub-tasks first.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are newly created; no modified or deleted test files |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies the comment directly as 'code change request' based on the reviewer's directive language ('The migration should also add an index'). It does not mention convention upgrade eligibility, convention analysis, or whether the index suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention. The report (line 6) mentions 'index addition classified as convention gap (repo-specific, undocumented pattern)' in the Root-Cause Investigation row, but this is a root-cause classification, not a convention upgrade eligibility analysis for the review comment classification. There is no explicit evaluation of whether the suggestion qualifies for convention upgrade from suggestion to code change request.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies it as 'code change request' with 'Sub-task required: Yes'. subtask-30002.md exists with a complete sub-task description for adding the partial index. The sub-task was created regardless of path — it was classified directly as a code change request.
